title: MEDIUM: Webhook endpoint lacks rate limiting (brute-force key guessing)
description:
## Location
src/server.rs (router), src/webhook.rs (handle_webhook)

## Finding
The webhook endpoint (/hook/{persona}) has no rate limiting. The inbox_limiter (60 req/min per IP) is only applied inside handle_inbox(), not to webhook requests.

An attacker who knows a persona name can attempt unlimited key guesses against /hook/{persona}. The constant-time comparison prevents timing-based attacks, but the lack of rate limiting allows high-throughput online brute force.

## Impact
If webhook keys are short or low-entropy, an attacker can enumerate them. Even with strong keys, the lack of rate limiting means the server absorbs arbitrary POST traffic to this endpoint with no backpressure.

## Recommendation
Apply rate limiting to the webhook endpoint — either reuse inbox_limiter or add a dedicated, tighter limiter (e.g., 10 req/min per IP). Consider returning 429 after repeated auth failures for the same persona.
close_reason: Delegated to reverse proxy. In-memory rate limiting doesn't survive restarts and is trivially bypassed by rotating IPs. README now documents Caddy rate_limit / nginx limit_req_zone configuration for webhook endpoints (10 req/min per IP). Matches smallhold's approach (commit 8f46fae).
